patterns: What are all the problem statements?

data.problems:
Problem 1: Environmental Risk Alert Application
Problem 2: Personal Medical History Vault
Problem 3: Smart Campus Services Hub
Problem 4: Document-Based Adaptive Quiz and Flashcard System

intents.name: problem_1_details
patterns: What is the first problem statement?
What is the environmental risk app?
What do we build for problem 1?
What is the environment problem statement?
Can you explain the first project?
What is the expected outcome for problem 1?

data.title: Problem 1: Environmental Risk Alert Application
data.objective: Build an application that monitors environmental conditions for a user's location and alerts the user only when conditions become harmful or dangerous.
data.requirements:
- Obtain environmental data (AQI, weather, UV index, temperature, traffic)
- Evaluate data to detect dangerous conditions
- Notify user only in severe cases (avoid unnecessary alerts)
- Display the reason for each alert
data.outcome: A working system that alerts users only during genuinely high-risk environmental conditions.

intents.name: problem_2_details
patterns: What is the second problem statement?
What is the medical history vault?
What do we build for problem 2?
What is the medical problem statement?
Details for personal medical history vault
Can you explain the second project?
What is the expected outcome for problem 2?

data.title: Problem 2: Personal Medical History Vault
data.objective: Build a secure app to organize all medical records, photos of medicines, prescriptions, and scan results in one place with smart categorization.
data.requirements:
- Upload photos/images of medicines, prescriptions, dental records, scans
- Use image recognition or manual tags to auto-categorize
- Store visit history with dates, doctors, notes, and enable search
- Provide reminders for expiring prescriptions or checkups
- Export records as PDF for doctors
data.outcome: A complete personal health archive that's easy to navigate and instantly accessible during emergencies.

intents.name: problem_3_details
patterns: What is the third problem statement?
What is the smart campus app?
What do we build for problem 3?
What is the campus problem statement?
Details for smart campus services hub
Can you explain the third project?
What is the expected outcome for problem 3?

data.title: Problem 3: Smart Campus Services Hub
data.objective: Build a unified app consolidating all non-academic campus services (canteen, maintenance, lost & found, transport) into one responsive platform.
data.requirements:
- Canteen pre-ordering with live kitchen status & pickup ETA
- Issue reporting: photo + GPS pin for fixes
- Lost & found: image upload + auto-notify finder
- Event discovery: department-wise push notifications
- Interactive campus map: you-are-here dot + pathfinding
data.outcome: Single app replaces 7 WhatsApp groups + manual processes, saving students 30+ minutes daily on campus logistics.

intents.name: problem_4_details
patterns: What is the fourth problem statement?
What do we build for problem 4?
What is the quiz problem statement?
Details for adaptive quiz and flashcard system
Can you explain the fourth project?
What is the expected outcome for problem 4?

data.title: Problem 4: Document-Based Adaptive Quiz and Flashcard System
data.objective: Allow users to upload slides, PDFs, assignments, notes, and question banks, and automatically convert them into quizzes and flashcards with spaced repetition.
data.requirements:
- Auto-extract questions/concepts from uploads to generate MCQs/flashcards
- Smart Retry: Incorrect questions reappear at spaced intervals
- Mastery Heatmap: Displays topic-wise performance
- Timed Quiz Mode & Weak Area Drill
- Export report with confidence scores
data.outcome: A system that converts uploaded academic material into structured quizzes and flashcards, monitors performance, and adapts practice.

intents.name: rules
patterns: Can I go home at night?
Do we have to use GitHub?

data.key_rules: Teams must create a new GitHub repo, push code at intervals, build an original solution, and stay on campus until the closing ceremony

response_templates: A few important rules: {key_rules}. Don't forget to bring your own laptops and a valid ID!
